title: `podman image ls repo/diskimg` always returns 0
body:
/kind bug

`podman image ls repo/diskimg` always returns code 0

This make things tough when trying to discover if an img exists

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman image ls
```
[fedora] [04:15:48 PM]
[ccallegar@~/git/kubevirt/website]$ podman image ls docker.io/netlify/build && echo $?
REPOSITORY               TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED      SIZE
docker.io/netlify/build  latest  3af6fd648ecb  3 weeks ago  4.44 GB
docker.io/netlify/build  xenial  3af6fd648ecb  3 weeks ago  4.44 GB
0

[fedora] [04:15:54 PM]
[ccallegar@~/git/kubevirt/website]$ podman image ls docker.io/netlify/beef && echo $?
REPOSITORY  TAG     IMAGE ID  CREATED  SIZE
0
```

**Describe the results you received:**
`podman image ls` always returns 0

**Describe the results you expected:**
I would expect return code 1 is search doesn't return sucessfully

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Fri Feb 19 08:56:17 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
VirtualBox guest

comments.body:
To discover if an image exists you could also use

 `podman image exists` 

 "_Check if an image exists in local storage_"
Quote from
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/docs/source/markdown/podman-image-exists.1.md

I tried it out:
```
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman --version
podman version 3.1.0
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman image exists docker.io/library/alpine:latest ; echo $?
1
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman image ls docker.io/library/alpine:latest ; echo $?
REPOSITORY  TAG     IMAGE ID  CREATED  SIZE
0
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman pull docker.io/library/alpine
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 540db60ca938 done  
Copying config 6dbb9cc540 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
6dbb9cc54074106d46d4ccb330f2a40a682d49dda5f4844962b7dce9fe44aaec
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman image exists docker.io/library/alpine:latest ; echo $?
0
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman image ls docker.io/library/alpine:latest ; echo $?
REPOSITORY                TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED     SIZE
docker.io/library/alpine  latest  6dbb9cc54074  2 days ago  5.88 MB
0
[erik@laptop ~]$ 
```

To me it makes sense to have `podman image ls` return 0 in case no images were found. That matches for instance the behaviour of  _/usr/bin/ls_:

```
[erik@laptop ~]$ mkdir /tmp/dir
[erik@laptop ~]$ /usr/bin/ls /tmp/dir ; echo $?
0
[erik@laptop ~]$ 
```

Doh, TIL!

Thanks for the help!
